evidence_quotes.quote: dark patterns, psychological manipulation, incentive conversion and many more nasty tactics
all games are forced to be online to function even if they don't need to.
video game industry such as EA, Ubisoft, Activision Blizzard
without any ability to take products/games away from the consumer for any reason.

paragraphs.text: I strongly urge that whoever is in power with "Digital Fairness Act" to cooperate with "Stop Killing Games initiative", and stop video game companies from destroying history, culture and illegally taking away legal purchases without any compensation or support. Over the past 20 years, major players in the video game industry such as EA, Ubisoft, Activision Blizzard and many more have made sure to go fully anti-consumer with their services and products by degrading, scamifying and even using hostile means for maximum profit by any means necessary! This is mostly done by a massive scam plan called "Live 2 service" where all games are forced to be online to function even if they don't need to. And then, by using dark patterns, psychological manipulation, incentive conversion and many more nasty tactics, traps are made for the victims that are quickly monetized, then the game is turned off, usually without warning, preferably killing it, so that victims are forced to move hopefully on to the next cycles of traps while effectively not having any evidence that the game even existed in the first place. Any game preservation or slightly possible monetization process deviation is attacked immediately. For instance, Nintendo sees everything as copyright infringement and piracy yet refuses to re-release old titles even if they are in demand and is willing to even sue individual consumers over nothing! Therefore, video game preservation is constantly under heavy fire for no good reason! THEY REPORTED ME. BYE or GOODBYE?... Once Were Nerd https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zSEB4if2pJQ&t=339s Darkpattern https://www.darkpattern.games/ Although I cannot find the infamous pro-dark pattern presentation where Evil game dev says that "That morality needs to be put aside", it does not take a lot of work to find what video game CEOs and investors think about gamers, developers and gaming in general. That's why I'm calling for the following: Video game companies must have a clear and understandable endplan for all of their products/games and must provide their products/games to the consumer who bought or invested in them legally without any ability to take products/games away from the consumer for any reason. Also, I would like to add that the payment process cannot deny you legal purchases and cannot be morality police that seeks to enforce their or someone's other will onto others. And that platforms like Steam or itch io cannot remove legally abiding games/products for no or ridiculous reasons, especially if customer purchased them and even if requested by a third entity. Yes, recently a group of right-wing extremists "Collective shout" with the help of corrupt government officials, seek to violently and undemocratically censor and police the internet while destroying anything they don't like! They use "protecting children and victims" as a meat shield for their real goals, which involves silencing the opposition, attacks on minorities and exploiting the already exploited. Companies involved such as Visa, mastercard, Steam and itch io bended immediately. And thanks to Uk Online Safety Act this cry bullying tactics is spreading to the US, Australia, Philippines and even EU. While the abused are disregarded as political leverage and sacrificial scapegoats that will stay used and abused. Don't Be Fooled By This https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4h3X37HLSPE Defend Democracy Now! Or Perish in the coming Autocracy!
